title: invitations — subscribed events
description: Cross-module reactions handled by the invitations module. The core invitation lifecycle events (InvitationSent, InvitationAccepted, InvitationRevoked, InvitationExpired) are PUBLISHED by this module — consumers listen to those. This file captures events that invitations SUBSCRIBES to from other modules.

listeners.purpose: On a fresh tenant, seed the owner's placeholder invitation record so the tenant's invite-history view has a start-state entry. Idempotent: no-op when the tenant was created by an existing user rather than a fresh signup.
Cascade-delete outstanding invitations for the erased tenant. Cascade FK already handles the DB rows; this listener writes the compliance audit trail entry + emits count metric.
When an inviter is deleted, revoke any pending invitations they issued so the invitee can't accept an invitation from a non-existent user. Sets state=revoked with `revoke_reason='inviter_removed'`.

When a user verifies their email (post-signup or on email change), auto-claim any pending invitations targeting that email so they surface in the user's dashboard without a manual `?token=...` follow-through. Debounced at 5s to survive rapid retries.
When a notifications::NotificationDelivered event fires with a source_module=invitations tag, write a matching InvitationEvent row for the audit trail (delivery timestamp + provider message id).
